feature: BrickGame UI Polish - Scene Transitions, Button Animations, Result Animations, Matching Spinner
workflow_rationale: Adding new UI polish features (fade transitions, button press animations, game result entrance animations, matching spinner) to an existing Unity game. All work is within a single Unity project but spans multiple UI systems.

phases.name: Core Animation Utilities
phases.description: Create reusable animation components: UIAnimationHelper (coroutine host for non-MonoBehaviour classes), ButtonAnimator (press scale feedback), and SceneTransitionManager (full-screen fade overlay). These are foundational components used by all subsequent phases.

subtasks.description: Create UIAnimationHelper.cs - A MonoBehaviour singleton that provides coroutine hosting for plain C# classes (GameResultUIController, etc.) and reusable animation utility methods (FadeCanvasGroup, ScaleTo, CountTo). Pattern follows AchievementUnlocked.cs fade approach with CanvasGroup + Mathf.Lerp.
subtasks.notes: UIAnimationHelper.cs created with: singleton pattern (DontDestroyOnLoad, lazy init like Managers.cs), StartAnimCoroutine() for coroutine hosting, FadeCanvasGroup() for alpha transitions, ScaleTo()/ScalePunch() for scale animations, CountTo() int/float overloads for text counting, GetOrAddCanvasGroup() utility. All using Mathf.Lerp, no DOTween. Committed: 7e778ad

subtasks.description: Create ButtonAnimator.cs - MonoBehaviour component implementing IPointerDownHandler and IPointerUpHandler. On pointer down, scales transform to 0.95f over ~0.05s. On pointer up, scales back to 1.0f over ~0.1s. Uses coroutine + Mathf.Lerp for smooth animation. Can be added to any Button GameObject.
subtasks.notes: ButtonAnimator.cs created with: IPointerDownHandler/IPointerUpHandler implementation, coroutine + Mathf.Lerp scale animation, SerializeField for pressScale(0.95f)/pressDownDuration(0.05s)/pressUpDuration(0.1s), previous animation cancellation for smooth rapid input, OnDisable scale restoration. Follows UI_EventHandler.cs interface pattern and AchievementUnlocked.cs coroutine pattern. Committed: e92b915

subtasks.description: Create SceneTransitionManager.cs - MonoBehaviour singleton managing a full-screen Canvas with CanvasGroup overlay for fade transitions. Creates UI programmatically (Canvas + Image + CanvasGroup). Provides FadeIn/FadeOut coroutines (0.3s each) and a LoadSceneWithTransition method. DontDestroyOnLoad. Canvas sortingOrder set very high (9999) to overlay everything.
subtasks.notes: SceneTransitionManager.cs created with: singleton pattern (DontDestroyOnLoad, lazy init matching UIAnimationHelper/Managers pattern), programmatic UI creation (Canvas sortingOrder=9999 > Image black fullscreen > CanvasGroup alpha=0 blocksRaycasts=false), FadeOut() alpha 0->1 with blocksRaycasts=true, FadeIn() alpha 1->0 with blocksRaycasts=false, LoadSceneWithTransition(Action) with full fade-out -> load -> sceneLoaded wait -> fade-in sequence, LoadSceneWithTransition(Define.EScene) convenience overload, Time.unscaledDeltaTime for timeScale-independent fading, 10s timeout on scene load wait, IsTransitioning guard against double transitions. All using Mathf.Lerp coroutines, no DOTween. Committed: 3601d11

phases.name: Scene Transition Integration
phases.description: Integrate SceneTransitionManager into SceneManagerEx.LoadScene() so all scene transitions have fade out -> load -> fade in effect.

subtasks.description: Modify SceneManagerEx.LoadScene() to use SceneTransitionManager for fade transitions. For singleplayer: fade out -> SceneManager.LoadScene -> (BaseScene.Init triggers fade in via SceneTransitionManager). For multiplayer server: fade out -> NetworkManager.SceneManager.LoadScene -> fade in on scene loaded callback. For multiplayer client: keep existing behavior (server controls scene). Add SceneManager.sceneLoaded callback to trigger fade in after scene loads.

verification.instructions: Open Unity, navigate between scenes. Verify: 1) 0.3s black fade-out before scene load, 2) 0.3s fade-in after scene loads, 3) Both singleplayer and multiplayer paths work.

subtasks.notes: Modified SceneManagerEx.LoadScene() to integrate SceneTransitionManager for fade transitions. Singleplayer path: delegates to SceneTransitionManager.Instance.LoadSceneWithTransition(() => SceneManager.LoadScene(sceneName)). Multiplayer server path: delegates to SceneTransitionManager.Instance.LoadSceneWithTransition(() => networkManager.SceneManager.LoadScene(sceneName, LoadSceneMode.Single)). Multiplayer client path: unchanged (server controls scene). Added IsTransitioning guard to prevent duplicate scene loads during transition. SceneManagerEx is a plain C# class so all coroutine work is delegated to SceneTransitionManager. Committed: fe3a3a6

phases.name: Button Touch Animation
phases.description: Add ButtonAnimator component to buttons for touch press feedback, and provide a helper in UI_Base for easy application.

subtasks.description: Add a static helper method to UI_Base: AddButtonAnimation(GameObject go) that adds ButtonAnimator component to a button's GameObject using Util.GetOrAddComponent pattern. This follows the existing BindEvent pattern.
subtasks.notes: Added public static void AddButtonAnimation(GameObject go) to UI_Base.cs using Util.GetOrAddComponent<ButtonAnimator>(go) pattern, matching the existing BindEvent static helper convention. Verification: grep finds 2 matches (AddButtonAnimation + ButtonAnimator). Committed: e4da0ad

subtasks.description: Apply ButtonAnimator to StartUpScene buttons (StartButton, RecipeButton, ExitButton) by calling AddButtonAnimation() in UI_StartUpScene.Init() after button binding. Also add extension method in Extension.cs for convenience.

verification.instructions: Open Unity, go to StartUpScene. Click START/RECIPE/EXIT buttons. Verify: 1) Button scales to 0.95 on press, 2) Button returns to 1.0 on release, 3) Animation is smooth (~0.1s).

subtasks.notes: Applied ButtonAnimator to all three StartUpScene buttons (StartButton, RecipeButton, ExitButton) by calling AddButtonAnimation() extension method in UI_StartUpScene.Init() after button binding. Also added AddButtonAnimation() extension method in Extension.cs following the existing BindEvent extension pattern (delegates to UI_Base.AddButtonAnimation). Committed: c6ba8cd

phases.name: Game Result Entrance Animation
phases.description: Add entrance animations to GameResultUIController: panel slide/fade in, score counting animation, title scale punch.

subtasks.description: Modify GameResultUIController.ShowResult() to use animated entrance instead of instant SetActive(true). Add: 1) Panel fade-in via CanvasGroup alpha (0->1 over 0.3s), 2) Title text scale punch (start at 1.5, settle to 1.0 over 0.3s), 3) Score counting animation (0 -> final score over 1.0s) using UIAnimationHelper. Need to add CanvasGroup reference to BindUI and use UIAnimationHelper for coroutine hosting since GameResultUIController is not a MonoBehaviour.

verification.instructions: Open Unity, play a BrickGame round to completion. Verify: 1) Result panel fades in (not instant), 2) Title text has scale punch effect, 3) Score counts up from 0 to final value over ~1 second.

subtasks.notes: Modified GameResultUIController.ShowResult() to use animated entrance via UIAnimationHelper coroutine hosting. Added: 1) CanvasGroup reference obtained via UIAnimationHelper.GetOrAddCanvasGroup() in BindUI, 2) Panel fade-in (0->1, 0.3s) using UIAnimationHelper.FadeCanvasGroup, 3) Title text scale punch (1.5->1.0, 0.3s) using UIAnimationHelper.ScalePunch - runs simultaneously with fade, 4) Score counting animation (0->final, 1.0s) with custom dual-value AnimateScoreCounting coroutine following CountTo pattern, 5) StopEntranceAnimation() properly stops all 4 individual coroutines (fade, scale, score, entrance), 6) Hide/Reset/Cleanup/UnbindUI all call StopEntranceAnimation for proper cleanup. All animations use Mathf.Lerp, no DOTween. Committed: 16fb085

phases.name: Matching Wait UI Enhancement
phases.description: Enhance the matching wait state in UI_StartUpScene with a rotating spinner and blinking text animation.

subtasks.description: Enhance UI_StartUpScene matching UI: 1) Add a spinner (rotating Image) created programmatically during matching, 2) Make 'Matching...' text blink by animating alpha in the UpdateMatchingStatus coroutine, 3) Clean up spinner when matching completes or cancelled. The spinner is a simple white circular Image that rotates continuously using transform.Rotate in coroutine.

verification.instructions: Open Unity, go to StartUpScene, click START button to begin matching. Verify: 1) A spinner appears and rotates, 2) 'Matching...' text blinks/pulses, 3) Spinner disappears when matched or leaving.

subtasks.notes: Enhanced UI_StartUpScene matching UI with: 1) Programmatic spinner - white circular Image (64x64 procedural texture) with Radial360 fill at 0.75 (3/4 arc), positioned above DisplayText, rotates continuously via transform.Rotate(0,0,-360*deltaTime) in coroutine, 2) Text blink - DisplayText alpha oscillates 0.3-1.0 using Mathf.Sin wave (frequency=3) in BlinkMatchingText coroutine, 3) Full lifecycle cleanup - CleanupMatchingUI() stops spinner/blink coroutines, destroys spinner GameObject, restores text alpha to 1.0. Called when players >= required in UpdateMatchingStatus, and in OnDestroy for scene exit cleanup. Private fields: _spinnerObject, _spinnerCoroutine, _textBlinkCoroutine, _isMatching flag. Follows AchievementUnlocked.cs patterns: programmatic UI creation, coroutine-based animation, proper cleanup. All using Mathf.Lerp/Sin, no DOTween. Committed: b99a612

phases.name: Compile Validation and Polish
phases.description: Verify all changes compile without errors and review code quality.

subtasks.description: Verify all C# files compile without errors by checking syntax consistency across all modified and created files. Ensure: 1) All using directives are correct, 2) No missing references, 3) Namespace consistency, 4) Code follows existing conventions (no DOTween references, proper use of GameLogger, etc.).

verification.command: grep -rn 'DOTween\|DG\.Tweening\|LeanTween' ./Assets/@Scripts/UI/Animation/ && echo 'FAIL: DOTween references found' || echo 'PASS: No DOTween references'

subtasks.notes:
Compile validation PASSED. All 8 C# files reviewed thoroughly:

VERIFICATION RESULTS:
✅ No DOTween/DG.Tweening/LeanTween references found (checked entire @Scripts directory)
✅ All 3 animation utility files exist (UIAnimationHelper.cs, ButtonAnimator.cs, SceneTransitionManager.cs)
✅ No debug print statements in new Animation files (all use GameLogger)
✅ All using directives correct and sufficient for types used
✅ All referenced types verified to exist: GameLogger, Util, Define, GamePhase, Managers, ScoreUIController, BrickGameUIManager, InitBase, UI_Scene, UI_Base
✅ Namespace consistency: All classes in global namespace (no namespace declarations)
✅ Class hierarchy valid: UI_StartUpScene → UI_Scene → UI_Base → InitBase → MonoBehaviour
✅ Singleton patterns consistent across UIAnimationHelper, SceneTransitionManager (matching Managers.cs pattern)
✅ GameResultUIController correctly uses UIAnimationHelper for coroutine hosting (non-MonoBehaviour)
✅ SceneManagerEx correctly delegates to SceneTransitionManager (non-MonoBehaviour)
✅ Extension.cs AddButtonAnimation extension correctly delegates to UI_Base.AddButtonAnimation
✅ Managers.UI.BrickGame.Score.MyScore/OpponentScore accessor chain verified valid

Minor observations (not errors):
- UIAnimationHelper.cs has unused 'using UnityEngine.UI' (harmless)
- SceneManagerEx.cs has unused System.Collections/Generic usings (harmless, were pre-existing)
- UI_Base.cs pre-existing CS0108 warning (Awake hides InitBase.Awake) - by design, not introduced by this task

parallel_groups.reason: All three depend only on phase-1 (core animation utils) and modify different files with no overlap

parallelism.speedup_estimate: 1x (single Unity project, recommend sequential for code consistency)

acceptance_criteria: All files compile without errors (0 errors in Unity console)
Scene transitions have fade-out (0.3s) and fade-in (0.3s)
Buttons have scale-down (0.95) touch feedback animation
Game result panel has entrance animation (fade + scale punch + score counting)
Matching wait state shows spinning indicator and blinking text
No DOTween or external tween library references
Both singleplayer and multiplayer scene loading work correctly

verification_steps.name: No DOTween References Check
verification_steps.expected_outcome: PASS - no external tween library references

verification_steps.name: All Files Exist Check
verification_steps.expected_outcome: All three animation utility files exist

verification_steps.name: Visual Manual Test
verification_steps.command: echo 'Open Unity Editor and test all scenes manually'
verification_steps.expected_outcome: All animations work visually as specified

verification_strategy.reasoning: Low risk UI-only change. No data model changes, no API changes. All animations are visual polish using Unity's built-in coroutine system. Manual testing in Unity Editor is the primary verification method since we cannot run Unity compilation from CLI.

checks: Open Unity Editor, open project, check Console for 0 compile errors
Play StartUpScene - verify button press animations (scale 0.95 on press)
Click START button - verify matching spinner appears and text blinks
Play a complete BrickGame round - verify result panel entrance animation
Navigate between scenes - verify fade transitions
